title: Berlin Conference Speakers
description: You are given three tables: conferences with a city, speakers, and presentations linking speakers to conferences. Find distinct names of speakers who presented at a conference held in 'Berlin', ordered alphabetically.
inputFormat:
conferences(id, name, city)
speakers(id, name, country)
presentations(id, speaker_id, conference_id, title)
outputFormat: One speaker name per line, ordered alphabetically. No duplicates.
constraints: Use two JOINs and DISTINCT. Filter WHERE city = 'Berlin'. Include ORDER BY name. Only SELECT queries are allowed.
sampleSolution: SELECT DISTINCT s.name FROM speakers s JOIN presentations p ON s.id = p.speaker_id JOIN conferences c ON p.conference_id = c.id WHERE c.city = 'Berlin' ORDER BY s.name;
hint: Join speakers → presentations → conferences, filter by city = 'Berlin', use DISTINCT.

testCases.setupSql: CREATE TABLE conferences (id INTEGER, name TEXT, city TEXT); CREATE TABLE speakers (id INTEGER, name TEXT, country TEXT); CREATE TABLE presentations (id INTEGER, speaker_id INTEGER, conference_id INTEGER, title TEXT); INSERT INTO conferences VALUES (1,'TechSummit','Berlin'),(2,'DataWorld','Paris'),(3,'AIConf','Berlin'); INSERT INTO speakers VALUES (1,'Alice','DE'),(2,'Bob','FR'),(3,'Carol','US'),(4,'Dan','UK'); INSERT INTO presentations VALUES (1,1,1,'ML Basics'),(2,2,2,'Stats 101'),(3,3,1,'Deep Learning'),(4,3,3,'Neural Nets'),(5,4,2,'Privacy');

CREATE TABLE conferences (id INTEGER, name TEXT, city TEXT); CREATE TABLE speakers (id INTEGER, name TEXT, country TEXT); CREATE TABLE presentations (id INTEGER, speaker_id INTEGER, conference_id INTEGER, title TEXT); INSERT INTO conferences VALUES (1,'BerlinOpen','Berlin'),(2,'LondonTech','London'),(3,'BerlinAI','Berlin'); INSERT INTO speakers VALUES (1,'Eva','DE'),(2,'Frank','UK'),(3,'Grace','US'),(4,'Hank','DE'); INSERT INTO presentations VALUES (1,1,1,'Robotics'),(2,2,2,'Cloud'),(3,3,2,'DevOps'),(4,4,3,'Vision'),(5,1,3,'NLP');
